content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES CO¥MISSION 
• 1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Michael Guina, City Attorney 
City of Emeryville 
City Attorney's Office 
1333 Park Avenue 
Emeryville, CA 94608 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-044 
Dear Mr. Guina: 
March 30, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of former Emeryville Planning 
Commissioner Phil Banta regarding the conflict ofinterest provisions of the Political Reform Act 
(the "Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions 
of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict 
of interest or Section l 0902• 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. Additionally, the Commission does not advise about past conduct. (Regulation 
18329(b)(8).) Therefore, nothing in this advice letter should be construed to advise about any 
conduct that has already taken place. 
QUESTION 
Does the Act, prohibit former Planning Commissioner Banta, or his firm, from participating 
as a design professional member of a team that submits a response to the City's RFQ/P, and that 
may eventually enter into a lease disposition and development agreement with the City for the 
design, construction and operation of the Art Center? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless othenvise indicated. 
2 Although you have not asked about Section 1090, and we have not analyzed whether Mr. Banta's 
participation in the discussions of the RFQ/P would be sufficient to preclude the City and Mr. Banta from entering into 
a contract, we note that resignation may not be sufficient to avoid a Section 1090 violation when the person has been 
involved in the contracting process. (See Stigall v. City of Taft (1962)58 Cal.2d 565). If you have concerns regarding 
Section 1090, you may wish to contact us for further advice. 


CONCLUSION 
File No. A-18-044 
Page No. 2 
Mr. Banta is not subject to the Act's conflict of interest rules because he has resigned from 
his position and will play no further official role in governmental decision making. 
FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR 
Mr. Banta is a licensed architect and owns an architectural firm. Mr. Banta's firm is 
incorporated with Mr. Banta as the sole owner. Through the corporation, Mr. Banta employs staff 
who assist him in representing clients, and who work with clients, subject to his direction. 
In February 2016, the Emeryville City Council appointed Mr. Banta to the City's Planning 
Commission. The Planning Commission serves as both the City's Planning Commission and design 
review/architectural review committee. There is no requirement that members of the Planning 
Commission include a licensed architect. On February 28, 2018, Mr. Banta submitted a letter 
resigning from his position of Planning Commissioner, with the resignation effective on February 
28, 2018, after serving for two years on the Planning Commission. 
On February 12, 2018, the City's Economic Development and Housing Division issued a 
Request for Qualifications/Proposals ("RFQ/P") for the Art Center. Mr. Banta was a Planning 
Commissioner at the time of issuance of the RFQ/P.3 The purpose of the RFQ/P is to solicit 
proposals from the development and arts community in which teams comprised of members from 
both development and the arts communities (''Teams") would compete to deliver a proposal for the 
capital construction, operation and maintenance of an Art Center that best furthers the City's 
objectives. The RFQ/P is a two-step competitive process. First, the Council will select those bidders 
deemed qualified to develop a proposal for the Art Center based on submittals from the Teams, then 
once Teams are deemed qualified, the qualified Teams compete for selection by submitting 
proposals. Once a proposal has been selected by the City Council, the qualified Team of the 
selected proposal and the City will enter into an exclusive right to negotiate period, in which the 
parties develop the terms and conditions for a lease disposition and development agreement, which 
will govern the design, construction and operation of the proposed Art Center on City owned 
property. 
It is anticipated that the Planning Commission would make a recommendation to the City 
Council regarding the lease disposition and development agreement. The Planning Commission 
also will make a recommendation to the City Council for approval regarding the design review for 
the Art Center once the Team selected seeks permits to construct the Art Center. The City Council 
will issue the design review permit. Once the design review permit has issued, the Team will need 
to obtain a building permit from the Chief Building Official. 
3 You also note that on or around January 31,2018, Mr. Banta had a meeting with EDHD Manager, the Mayor 
and the Chair of the Public Art Committee at Mr. Banta's office in the City to discuss the RFQ/P. At the meeting, Mr. 
Banta volunteered that it could be worth loosening the requirements of the RFQIP. 


ANALYSIS 
File No. A-18-044 
Page No. 3 
The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit a public official from taking part in a 
governmental decision in which the official has a conflict of interest. These conflict of interest 
provisions are designed to ensure that "(p ]ublic officials, whether elected or appointed, perform 
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests ... " 
(Section 81001(b).) Thus, the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, 
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which he or 
she has a financial interest. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) 
The Act's conflict of interest rules operate throughout the period when a public servant can 
take part in governmental decision making. You indicate that Mr. Banta has resigned from the 
Planning Commission and will play no further official role in governmental decision making and, 
so long as that is the case, his private-sector activities will not give rise to a conflict of interest 
under the Act. Although the Act's conflict of interest rules are not applicable in this case, it is 
important to recognize that when a government employee is no longer engaged in the duties of his 
or her public office, and is employed in the private sector, his or her actions may become subject to 
the Act's post-governmental employment restrictions. 
Local One-year Ban 
The Act contains two revolving door provisions (Sections 87406.1 and 87406.3) that 
address specified one-year prohibitions for local governmental officials who leave governmental 
service. Section 87406.3 applies to elected officials and chief administrative officers or general 
managers of a county, city, or special district and generally prohibits them from appearing before or 
communicating with their former agency for the purpose of influencing any legislative or 
administrative actions, including quasi-legislative and quasi-judicial actions. Section 87406.1 
applies to former members of air pollution control district boards, among others, and generally 
prohibits them from appearing before or communicating with their former agency for the purpose of 
influencing regulatory action. As Mr. Banta formerly served as an appointed member of the City's 
Planning Commission, he is not subject to the restrictions of the local one-year ban. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: A ,01~ --
~
1ary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
ZWN:jgl
